# Sub 3: Layer 3 — per-turn anti-pattern surface (AGENTS.md + peer-role-mode.md)

## Parent Epic

#11829 — Sub 3 of 5.

## Premise

Per Epic AC4: even with Layer 1 (content-rich wake) + Layer 2 (cross-family delivery), agents may still misinterpret "stale wake = idle cover" — the anti-pattern needs explicit per-turn surface so it's caught at the discipline tier.

Empirical anchor: 2026-05-24 session showed me (Claude Opus 4.7) repeatedly marking stale wakes as "no action" while ZERO own-PRs were shipping (operator-flagged velocity-gap). The discipline needs per-turn anchor.

## Prescription

Two surface updates (compact, NO new audit file / template surface per `#11890` why-first contract):

1. **AGENTS.md §edge_case_triggers** — new bullet:
   *"Wake/Heartbeat-without-active-lifecycle: query unassigned current-release Project; halt only at zero-candidate state. Exhausted-self-assigned-bench is NOT a halt-state."*

2. **peer-role-mode.md §7 Anti-Pattern Catalog** — new row:
   *"Stale-wake silent-mark-read pattern: marking heartbeats / stale-event wakes as 'no action' while ZERO own-PRs ship is deference-slip dressed as discipline. Trigger: query unassigned current-release backlog OR confirm zero-candidate halt with named reason."*

Compact-only — per #11890 / #11894 / #11897 / #11898 substrate-evolution discipline: extend existing prose, do NOT add new audit file or template section.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `AGENTS.md §edge_case_triggers` bullet added per template above
- [ ] AC2: `peer-role-mode.md §7` anti-pattern row added per template above
- [ ] AC3: No new audit file / template surface / ADR created
- [ ] AC4: lint-skill-manifest --base origin/dev passes (no oversized-workflow-map delta breach)
- [ ] AC5: Empirical anchor (this session's stale-wake pattern, 2026-05-24) cited in commit body for substrate-evolution provenance

## Avoided Traps (per Epic + #11890 contract)

- ❌ New audit file / template section to enforce discipline — operator-rejected pattern (additive-drift reflex)
- ❌ Hardcoded substrate ticket references in the rule text (per #11890 source-code-anchor discipline)

## Depends on

None — independent of Subs 1/2 (different substrate surface)

## Related

- Epic #11829 (parent, AC4)
- #11890 / PR #11897 (compact-only why-first contract — discipline this sub adheres to)
- `peer-role-mode.md §7` (target surface)
- `AGENTS.md §edge_case_triggers` (target surface)
- This session's stale-wake-as-deference-slip pattern (2026-05-24 ~13:18Z operator velocity-gap callout)

**Authored by:** Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code)

- 2026-05-24T14:38:47Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `20e46f8` - "docs(agentos): strip ticket/epic anchors from wake-anti-pattern hot-path (#11907)

GPT review on PR #11910 cycle-1 flagged the loaded rule text carrying ticket
+ epic + history anchors instead of durable intent. Per the why-first
direction shipped with #11890 hot-path text holds intent, not provenance
(provenance lives in PR/ticket/commit context).

- AGENTS.md §edge_case_triggers: drop `(Epic #11829 Layer 3)` anchor.
- peer-role-mode.md §7: drop `(per #11907 / Epic #11829 Layer 3)` anchor and
  the multi-sentence empirical anchor block; add the next-lifecycle-event
  prompt clause that makes the intent self-contained.

Resulting rule text is provenance-free + still self-contained.
